items.title: Slot legt uit waarom Gakpo deze goal nodig had
items.summary: Arne Slot was niet alleen blij met de ruime zege van Liverpool op West Ham United, maar in het bijzonder met de goal die landgenoot Cody Gakpo maakte. De Nederlander verkeert in een moeilijke periode en kon de treffer volgens Slot goed gebruiken.

items.title: Van de Ven aangepakt in Engeland: 'Dit kan hem een transfer naar topclub kosten'
items.summary: Micky van de Ven ligt onder vuur in Engeland na de nederlaag van Tottenham Hotspur bij Fulham. De Oranje-international droeg bij afwezigheid van Cristian Romero de aanvoerdersband, maar kreeg na afloop stevige kritiek. Oud-international Tony Cascarino spaarde hem niet in The Times, terwijl ook de London Evening Standard een onvoldoende uitdeelde.

items.title: Arteta: 'Heart almost stopped' on late Raya save
items.summary: Mikel Arteta hailed David Raya's performance in Arsenal's 2-1 win against Chelsea after saying his "heart almost stopped" before the goalkeeper made a crucial stoppage time save to keep the Gunners in pole position in the Premier League title race.

items.title: Mourinho: 'Over' for Prestianni if guilty of abuse
items.summary: Jose Mourinho said Gianluca Prestianni will have no future at Benfica if he is found guilty of racially abusing Real Madrid's Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: United got 'Old Trafford bonus' in win - Palace boss
items.summary: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner has branded the decision to award Manchester United a penalty in their 2-1 defeat on Sunday an "Old Trafford bonus."

items.title: Tudor slams 'cheating' Fulham after Spurs loss
items.summary: Tottenham interim head coach Igor Tudor took aim at his own players before he slammed "cheating" Fulham after another defeat.

items.title: England 'given reassurances' over safety in Turkey
items.summary: Sarina Wiegman says England have been told there are no safety concerns over their World Cup qualifier in Turkey despite the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Slot keek met verbazing naar moment in duel tussen Man Utd en Crystal Palace
items.summary: Arne Slot heeft met verbazing gekeken naar de rode kaart van Maxence Lacroix tijdens Manchester United tegen Crystal Palace . Voor exact hetzelfde moment kreeg Marc Guéhi eerder deze maand geen rode kaart voor Manchester City in het duel met Liverpool. Slot laakt de arbitrale willekeur.

items.title: Elftal van de Week: vier clubs leveren ieder twee spelers af
items.summary: Het was deze week iets uitdagender dan normaal om een Elftal van de Week samen te stellen. Dat had niets te maken met slechte beoordelingen, maar juist met goede. Er is geen hofleverancier, wel vier clubs die ieder twee spelers afleveren.

items.title: Rondje Eredivisie: Feyenoord en Ajax falen, hoeveel tijd heeft Van Persie nog?
items.summary: Na afloop van speelronde 25 in de Eredivisie is er weer genoeg om na te bespreken. Vooral de nog altijd spannende strijd om plek twee houdt de gemoederen bezig. Schakel om 10.30 uur in voor Rondje Eredivisie.

items.title: Important goals and big saves: Who were the weekend's difference makers?
items.summary: Matchweek 28 gave us heroics by the bucketload. Sky Sports picks out the peak Premier League performers who produced the standout moments.

items.title: England keeping close eye on safety before World Cup qualifier in Turkey
items.summary: Lionesses face Ukraine in Antalya on Tuesday Wiegman says ‘reassurances’ received before trip Sarina Wiegman says England’s women’s team have had reassurances they are safe in Turkey but remain in close contact with the authorities about the developing conflict in the Middle East, as they prepare to face Ukraine in a Women’s World Cup qualifier. Tuesday’s match is being played in Antalya, along Turkey’s southern Mediterranean coast, on neutral ground because of the war in Ukraine. The Lionesses travelled to Antalya to begin a training camp last week, landing before the war began in the Middle East. Continue reading...

items.title: Ref Watch LIVE! 'Chelsea denied penalty' but Lacroix red splits panel
Was Chermiti's overhead kick best Old Firm goal this century?
items.summary: Is Youssef Chermiti's overhead kick for Rangers against Celtic the best of the 2000s so far? Or do some of the other strikes in the fixture get your vote?

items.title: VAR review: Did Chelsea deserve penalty in loss to Arsenal?
items.summary: It appeared Arsenal's Declan Rice had intentionally hit the ball with his arm, and the call could've changed the game for Chelsea. Was the no-call correct?

items.title: Premier League: 10 talking points from the weekend’s action
items.summary: Jordan Pickford’s ‘best save ever’, Antoine Semenyo’s shifting mentality and Liverpool’s set-piece threat grows Premier League top scorers: check the latest standings Arsenal won the battle of set pieces, beating Chelsea 2-1 to keep Manchester City at bay. In a game that offered few clearcut chances from open play, it was a familiar story of Arsenal overpowering their opponents from corner kicks. Gabriel bullied Reece James to set up William Saliba for their first goal and Jur​riën Timber punished a flailing Robert Sánchez for their second. Mikel Arteta’s side have equalled the record for the most goals scored from corners in a Premier League season (16) with nine games still to go. Meanwhile, Chelsea have conceded seven goals from set pieces in Liam Rosenior’s first 13 games in all competitions. Despite posing a threat offensively through Reece James’s delivery for Piero Hincapié’s own goal, they repeatedly failed to match Arsenal’s physicality when defending. items.title: What can players and clubs do about 'AI slop'?
items.summary: There is an exponential growth of AI 'slop' involving players and football clubs. Is there anything that can be done to stop it?

items.title: Ukraine's Kozlova: 'We're playing for something more than ourselves'
items.summary: Taking on the European champions should be one of the happiest moments of a player’s career, but for Nicole Kozlova, it’s also tinged with sadness.

items.title: 'Where did that come from?!' | Lowry finds water TWICE in successive holes to blow lead
items.summary: Having led by three, Shane Lowry found the water with his tee shot at the 16th and 17th holes as he blew his chance of winning the Cognizant Classic.

items.title: 'Ugly' or 'beautiful'? Set-pieces prove decisive for Arsenal again
items.summary: Arsenal move five points clear in the Premier League title race but is their reliance on set-pieces an issue?
